backend/main.mo: Motoko actor that stores patient health records in a map, calculates a weighted risk score from clinical parameters (age, BMI, blood pressure, glucose, hemoglobin, cholesterol, smoking), and exposes add/get query functions.
feature_evidence.json: Empty feature evidence report JSON file with no recorded features.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-to-two sentence summaries of each frontend source file, serving as a reference for the project's frontend structure.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base CSS file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark themes, covering background, foreground, card, sidebar, chart, and destructive color tokens.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point that mounts the React app into a root div and loads the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Query, TanStack Router with four routes (/, /xray, /dashboard, /history), a sidebar layout with header/footer, and a caffeine.ai attribution link.
frontend/src/components/AppSidebar.tsx: Sidebar navigation component with logo, four nav links (Risk Predictor, X-Ray Analysis, Dashboard, History), active-state highlighting, a medical disclaimer notice, and a footer copyright.
frontend/src/components/RiskGauge.tsx: Canvas-based semicircular gauge that renders color-coded arc segments, a needle, and a numeric score based on the risk score and RiskLevel enum from the backend interface.
Horizontal progress-bar chart component displaying Normal and Pneumonia probability percentages with green and red color coding.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches a backend actor instance via TanStack Query, using the current Internet Identity for authenticated or anonymous access, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication, managing login/logout state and persisting identity across page reloads using DFINITY's AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching all health records and adding a new record via the backend actor.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that imports fonts, sets up Tailwind directives, and defines CSS custom properties for a clinical navy/teal color palette including light/dark themes and risk-level utility classes.
frontend/src/lib/riskCalculator.ts: Implements a scoring function that calculates a 0–100 cardiovascular risk score from patient vitals and classifies it as low, moderate, or high risk.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Dashboard page that displays summary statistics (total, high-risk, moderate-risk counts, average score) and a sortable table of all health records with CSV export functionality.
frontend/src/pages/History.tsx: History page that lists all past risk assessments sorted by most recent, showing patient details, timestamps, and color-coded risk badges.
frontend/src/pages/RiskPredictor.tsx: Risk Predictor page with a form for entering patient vitals that computes a live risk score client-side and allows saving the result to the backend canister.
frontend/src/pages/XRayAnalysis.tsx: React page that allows users to upload a chest X-ray image via drag-and-drop or file input, runs a simulated pneumonia classification, and displays the results using a chart component.
frontend/src/utils/csvExport.ts: Utility function that converts an array of HealthRecord objects into a CSV file and triggers a browser download with a date-stamped filename.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration that sets up dark mode, custom color palettes (navy, teal, health status colors), typography, container queries, animation plugins, and extended design tokens like shadows and border radii.
project_state.json: Tracks the current state of the AI Healthcare Monitoring project, noting all features are working and recording a pending deployment/publish request (AR-1).
